coverage: 100% of reachable node types; Object Info now preserves its As Instance component so the final wrapper switch matches Blender.
validation: All six authored input cases match the recovered-Pixels.ttf Blender reference exactly at 161722 vertices, 88994 polygon faces, and rounded local bounds. Mesh to Curve preserves attached-loop closing edges by repeating their branch pole as Blender does. Realize Instances now excludes the VM-private __font_sample_stride marker, preventing a downstream Fill Curve from dissolving already-realized glyph samples a second time while retaining authored curve attributes. The complete 538,800-index surface uses flat.nodes. Browser evaluation preserves 158,950 black and 2,772 gray POINT-domain col values; all 105,172 vertices referenced by faces retain power=1, while 56,550 non-surface points retain power=0. The interactive viewport can expose the exact 3,226 curve points / 2,797 guide segments, but authored comparison hides those non-rendering lines. Its mesh-bounds mode retains Blender's unreferenced evaluated mesh vertices while excluding the separate Z=55 guide, reproducing the stored 92.997 × 92.045 × 50 camera bounds. The authored WebGL evidence now uses an 8-sample Eevee-style temporal resolve. The comparison composites Blender's straight-alpha image over the same WebGL key before applying one shared scene-linear threshold, avoiding the former alpha>0.5 versus color-key coverage mismatch. It reaches 0.940531 surface-mask IoU, 0.5594-pixel corner RMSE, 0.998706 macro luminance correlation, 0.955729 pixel luminance correlation, 0.061839 luminance MAE, and +0.004566 mean-luminance error. Remaining differences are subpixel raster coverage and internal edge interpolation, not geometry, camera framing, or the flat.nodes emission field.
